scope_boundary: Lean finite-record proof wiring only; no semantic proof adequacy, runtime behavior, or broad system guarantee is promoted
transition_reason: All 112 proof targets build as narrow finite-record predicates and are wired through the manifest and audit, but the roadmap still requires proof adequacy review. Lean build success proves only the checked predicates, not that the predicates are the right formalization of each intended chapter boundary.
required_artifacts: semantic proof adequacy review for all proof targets
classification of targets as adequate, too narrow, requiring tests first, or research agenda
chapter updates for any stronger proof interpretation
negative_results: proof adequacy review has not been performed
finite-record proofs do not prove broad runtime behavior
proof build does not promote source interpretation
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after proof adequacy review accepts a tightly scoped claim about proof wiring or formalized finite-record invariants and the claim text is narrowed accordingly.
limitations: Lean proves only the imported finite predicates
schemas validate shape and tests validate behavior in separate lanes
no runtime property follows from these proofs alone
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent formal-methods audit
acceptance_blockers: claim text remains broader than proof wiring
non_claims: does not claim broad proof of safety
does not claim the Lean targets are semantically adequate
does not promote any chapter claim
